meta.title: Electrical Panel Upgrades Charlotte NC | Johnson's Electric
meta.description: Licensed panel upgrade specialists in Charlotte, NC. Upgrade your outdated breaker box to a modern 200A or 400A panel. Safe, permitted, and inspected. Call (704) 555-0199.
meta.og_title: Electrical Panel Upgrades | Johnson's Electric of Charlotte
meta.og_description: Charlotte's trusted panel upgrade electricians. Fuse box replacement, 200A and 400A service upgrades, sub-panels: all permitted and inspected. Get a free estimate.

hero.label: Electrical Panel Upgrades
hero.heading: Charlotte's Panel Upgrade Specialists
hero.subheading: An outdated or undersized electrical panel is the most common cause of house fires in older homes. We upgrade your panel safely, efficiently, and fully permitted.

intro.label: Is It Time to Upgrade?
intro.heading: Warning Signs You Need a Panel Upgrade
intro.body1: Most Charlotte homes built before 1990 were wired for 100 amps or less, far short of the 200 amps that modern homes with electric appliances, EV chargers, HVAC systems, and home offices require. An undersized panel forces you to prioritize what you run at once, and aging breakers can fail to trip when they should.
intro.body2: Johnson's Electric specializes in panel upgrades for Charlotte-area homes and businesses. We assess your current and future electrical needs, recommend the right panel size, pull the permit, coordinate with Duke Energy, and handle the full installation, including the service entrance and grounding system.
intro.image_alt: Electrician upgrading an electrical panel in Charlotte NC

checklist.text: Breakers that trip frequently or won't reset
No room for new circuits in your panel
Adding an EV charger or hot tub
Burning smell or discoloration near the panel

services.label: Panel Services We Offer
services.heading: Complete Panel Upgrade Services
services.subheading: From simple panel replacements to full service entrance upgrades, we handle every type of panel work for Charlotte homes and businesses.

items.title: 200-Amp Service Upgrade
items.description: The most common upgrade, moving from an undersized 60A or 100A panel to a modern 200-amp service entrance. This gives your home ample capacity for all modern electrical demands.

items.title: 400-Amp Service Upgrade
items.description: Larger homes, home additions, or properties with multiple EV chargers, hot tubs, or heavy HVAC systems may need 400-amp service. We design and install 400A service entrance upgrades.

items.title: Fuse Box Replacement
items.description: Older homes with fuse boxes are not only inconvenient, they're a safety hazard and may make homeowner's insurance difficult or expensive to obtain. We replace fuse boxes with modern breaker panels.

items.title: Sub-Panel Installation
items.description: Adding a workshop, garage, ADU, or home addition? A sub-panel feeds a separate area of your property from your main panel, giving those spaces their own dedicated circuits without overloading your main panel.

items.title: Whole-Home Surge Protection
items.description: Modern panel upgrades are the perfect opportunity to add whole-home surge protection. A single surge protector installed at your panel shields every outlet, appliance, and electronic device in your home.

items.title: Permit & Inspection Handling
items.description: Every panel upgrade requires a permit and inspection by Charlotte-Mecklenburg Building Standards. We handle the entire process: permit application, inspection scheduling, and final approval.

benefits.label: Why Upgrade Your Panel
benefits.heading: The Benefits of a Modern Electrical Panel
benefits.subheading: A panel upgrade is one of the highest-value improvements you can make to your Charlotte home, for safety, convenience, and resale value.

items.title: Dramatically Improved Safety
items.description: Modern circuit breakers with AFCI and GFCI protection detect dangerous electrical faults and arc faults that older panels miss entirely. This is the most important reason to upgrade.

items.title: Meets Modern Electrical Demands
items.description: Today's homes have far more electrical loads than homes from 30 years ago. A 200A panel handles EV charging, modern HVAC systems, home offices, and large appliances without issue.

items.title: Supports Home Additions & Upgrades
items.description: Adding a room, a hot tub, or an EV charger? These all require additional circuits. A new panel gives you the headroom to expand without overloading your electrical system.

items.title: Increased Home Value
items.description: A modern panel is a selling point for Charlotte home buyers and required by some mortgage and insurance providers. A completed permit ensures proper documentation for your home's sale.

items.title: Homeowners Insurance Compliance
items.description: Many insurance companies refuse to cover, or charge significantly higher premiums for, homes with fuse boxes or specific outdated panel brands. A new panel can lower your insurance costs.

items.title: Peace of Mind
items.description: Knowing your electrical system is modern, code-compliant, and properly installed provides genuine peace of mind. You stop wondering if that flicker or tripping breaker is a warning sign.

process.label: How a Panel Upgrade Works
process.heading: Your Panel Upgrade Step by Step
process.subheading: A panel upgrade is a major electrical project, but with Johnson's Electric it's a smooth, well-coordinated process with minimal disruption to your day.
process.image_alt: Electrician completing a panel upgrade installation
process.highlight_text: Most residential panel upgrades are completed in a single day. Your power is typically off for just 2 to 4 hours during the service disconnect.

steps.title: In-Home Assessment & Load Analysis
steps.description: A licensed electrician visits your home to assess your current panel, calculate your electrical load, and recommend the right panel size for your current and future needs.

steps.title: Written Estimate & Permit Application
steps.description: We provide a detailed written estimate and immediately begin the permit application process once you approve the work. Permits are pulled before any work begins.

steps.title: Duke Energy Coordination
steps.description: We contact Duke Energy to schedule the required service disconnect. This brief outage (typically 1-2 hours) is required for the utility to safely disconnect power from the street.

steps.title: Panel Removal & Installation
steps.description: We remove your old panel and install the new one, including the service entrance conductors, main breaker, branch circuits, grounding, bonding, and arc-fault protection.

steps.title: Inspection & Final Sign-Off
steps.description: A city or county inspector verifies all work meets NEC and local code requirements. We schedule and attend the inspection on your behalf. You receive final permit documentation.

faq.label: Panel Upgrade FAQs
faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subheading: Answers to the questions Charlotte homeowners ask most often about electrical panel upgrades.

items.question: How much does a panel upgrade cost in Charlotte?
items.answer: A 200-amp panel upgrade in Charlotte typically costs between $1,500 and $3,500 depending on the existing wiring, service entrance condition, and whether sub-panel work is needed. We provide a detailed written estimate before starting any work.

items.question: How long will my power be off during the upgrade?
items.answer: Most of the project is completed with your power on. The brief period when power must be off - the service disconnect, is typically 1 to 2 hours while Duke Energy works on the meter and we swap the panel.

items.question: Do I need to be home during the panel upgrade?
items.answer: Yes, we recommend being home for the full day of the upgrade. You'll need to be available when Duke Energy arrives for the disconnect, and we may have questions about your electrical system during the installation.

items.question: Will a panel upgrade increase my home's value?
items.answer: Yes. A modern, permitted electrical panel is a significant positive for Charlotte home buyers and appraisers. Many buyers specifically look for homes with updated electrical systems, and some mortgage types require it.

items.question: What brands of panels do you install?
items.answer: We install major name brands including Square D (Homeline and QO), Eaton, and Siemens. We avoid certain brands that have documented safety issues and recall histories, and we're happy to discuss our recommendations with you.

items.question: Is my panel covered by a warranty?
items.answer: Yes. Panel hardware carries a manufacturer's warranty, and all our installation work is covered by our 1-year written workmanship warranty. If any installation issue arises within a year, we return to resolve it at no charge.

cta.label: Ready to Upgrade?
cta.heading: Get Your Free Panel Upgrade Estimate Today
cta.body: Don't wait for a breaker failure or an electrical fire to force the issue. Schedule a free in-home panel assessment with Johnson's Electric and get a written estimate.

checklist.text: Written estimate before any work begins
